mission.title: Install and Configure Cartography on Kubernetes
mission.description: Cartography is a Python tool that consolidates infrastructure assets and their relationships in an intuitive graph view powered by a Neo4j database. Installing Cartography allows you to visualize and manage your infrastructure's security risks effectively.

steps.title: Create a namespace for Cartography
steps.description:
First, create a dedicated namespace for Cartography to keep your resources organized. Run the following command:
```bash
kubectl create namespace cartography
```

steps.title: Deploy the Neo4j database
steps.description:
Deploy Neo4j, which is required for Cartography to function. Use the following command to apply the Neo4j deployment:
```bash
kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/cartography-cncf/cartography/main/deploy/neo4j-deployment.yaml --namespace cartography
```

steps.title: Deploy Cartography
steps.description:
Now, deploy Cartography itself. Use the following command to apply the Cartography deployment:
```bash
kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/cartography-cncf/cartography/main/deploy/cartography-deployment.yaml --namespace cartography
```

steps.title: Verify the installation
steps.description:
Check if the Cartography and Neo4j pods are running successfully:
```bash
kubectl get pods --namespace cartography
```

steps.title: Port-forward to access the Cartography web interface
steps.description:
To access the Cartography web interface, port-forward the service:
```bash
kubectl port-forward svc/cartography --namespace cartography 8080:80
```

steps.title: Post-install configuration
steps.description:
Configure resource limits for the Cartography deployment. Edit the deployment with:
```bash
kubectl edit deployment cartography --namespace cartography
``` 
Add resource limits under the containers section:
```yaml
resources:
  limits:
    memory: "512Mi"
    cpu: "500m"
```

uninstall.title: Remove the Cartography deployment
uninstall.description:
First, delete the Cartography deployment with:
```bash
kubectl delete deployment cartography --namespace cartography
```

uninstall.title: Remove the Neo4j deployment
uninstall.description:
Next, delete the Neo4j deployment with:
```bash
kubectl delete deployment neo4j --namespace cartography
```

uninstall.title: Clean up the namespace
uninstall.description:
Finally, delete the Cartography namespace to remove all associated resources:
```bash
kubectl delete namespace cartography
```

upgrade.title: Backup current state
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, back up your Neo4j database. You can use the following command:
```bash
kubectl exec -it <neo4j-pod-name> --namespace cartography neo4j-admin dump --to=/var/lib/neo4j/dump/backup.dump
```

upgrade.title: Update the deployment manifests
upgrade.description:
Fetch the latest version of the Cartography deployment YAML file and update your local copy:
```bash
curl -O https://raw.githubusercontent.com/cartography-cncf/cartography/main/deploy/cartography-deployment.yaml
```

upgrade.title: Upgrade the deployment
upgrade.description:
Apply the updated deployment manifest:
```bash
kubectl apply -f cartography-deployment.yaml --namespace cartography
```

upgrade.title: Verify the upgrade
upgrade.description:
Check the status of the pods to ensure the upgrade was successful:
```bash
kubectl get pods --namespace cartography
```

troubleshooting.title: Pods stuck in Pending state
troubleshooting.description:
If your pods are stuck in Pending, check for resource availability:
```bash
kubectl describe pod <pod-name> --namespace cartography
``` 
Ensure that your cluster has enough resources to schedule the pods.

troubleshooting.title: Neo4j not starting
troubleshooting.description:
If the Neo4j pod is not starting, check the logs for errors:
```bash
kubectl logs <neo4j-pod-name> --namespace cartography
``` 
Look for any configuration issues or missing environment variables.

troubleshooting.title: Cartography web interface not accessible
troubleshooting.description:
If you can't access the Cartography web interface, ensure the port-forward command is running and check the service:
```bash
kubectl get svc --namespace cartography
``` 
Make sure the service is correctly configured and running.

resolution.summary: A successful installation of Cartography will have both the Cartography and Neo4j pods running in the 'cartography' namespace. You should be able to access the Cartography web interface via port 8080.

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a Kubernetes cluster running and kubectl installed.
